Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

How is this not used?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2
> 
> How is this not used?

We have two CloudConfig classes, one is deprecated and currently not used, this is the one in the ticket. The second class - R__CloudConfigMigration.java is also not used. Do not worry, before I delete this code, I had checked carefully if it is not used.

Patch Set 3:

R__CloludConfigMigration, auto runs on start up of catalog db pod to populate the tables for cloud configuration.  I am not understanding why we would remove it?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> R__CloludConfigMigration, auto runs on start up of catalog db pod to populate the tables for cloud configuration.  I am not understanding why we would remove it?

Hi Steve, you wrote that this code is run automatically when catalog-db-adapter pod starts. I checked it, I see no line in logs that could confirm that, although logger messages are avaiable in the R__CloudConfigMigration.class.

When exactly it is invoked? Or maybe it is just old, not used implementation? Please check. Here you have logs when catalog db pod starts:

2019-06-04T10:21:02.483Z|| o.onap.so.adapters.catalogdb.CatalogDBApplication - Starting CatalogDBApplication on dev-so-so-catalog-db-adapter-67b6b74f45-xwjk6 with PID 16 (/app/app.jar started by so in /app)
2019-06-04T10:21:02.487Z|| o.onap.so.adapters.catalogdb.CatalogDBApplication - Running with Spring Boot v2.0.5.RELEASE, Spring v5.0.9.RELEASE
2019-06-04T10:21:02.522Z|| o.onap.so.adapters.catalogdb.CatalogDBApplication - No active profile set, falling back to default profiles: default
2019-06-04T10:21:07.476Z|| org.flywaydb.core.Flyway - Flyway.setCallbacks(FlywayCallback) has been deprecated and will be removed in Flyway 6.0. Use Flyway.setCallbacks(Callback) instead.
2019-06-04T10:21:08.064Z|| org.flywaydb.core.internal.command.DbMigrate - outOfOrder mode is active. Migration of schema `catalogdb` may not be reproducible.
2019-06-04T10:21:13.722Z|| o.s.b.a.o.j.JpaBaseConfiguration$JpaWebConfiguration$JpaWebMvcConfiguration - spring.jpa.open-in-view is enabled by default. Therefore, database queries may be performed during view re
2019-06-04T10:21:14.983Z|| o.onap.so.adapters.catalogdb.CatalogDBApplication - Started CatalogDBApplication in 13.202 seconds (JVM running for 13.812)

Patch Set 3:

What log level is set in the environment you are running this? can you turn on the logging for the flyway packages? by default they are at a very low level such as Warn.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> What log level is set in the environment you are running this? can you turn on the logging for the flyway packages? by default they are at a very low level such as Warn.

The ONAP logs are on debug level, from fhe degub.log file. I do not know the level of flyway logs, I see some warnings that the logic is deprecated.
However, I see that R__CloudConfigMigration class is the only place where flyway is used. If the flyway library messages are visible in mso-catalog-db logs, it means that R__CloudConfigMigration invokes the flyway logic. The class implements the interfaces from this library. 
Anyway, I abandon this ticket. The importance is low, and it requires deeper analysis if this flyaway library is still needed. Thanks Steve for review.
